compiled_state.title: As a developer, sprint dispatches RED tasks to the dedicated writer with escalation
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Modify the sprint orchestrator (SKILL.md) and task classification pipeline (classify-task.py, agent-profiles/) to route RED test tasks to `dso:red-test-writer` via direct `subagent_type` dispatch. Add the three-tier escalation protocol as a shared prompt template that Story 4 (fix-bug) consumes.

**Why**: Sprint is the primary orchestrator for TDD workflows. Routing RED tasks to the dedicated writer enforces behavioral test quality at the point of highest leverage.

**Scope**:
- IN: classify-task.py modification to route RED tasks, agent profile or pre-classification override, sprint SKILL.md escalation protocol, shared escalation prompt template, dispatch failure recovery override for RED tasks, test-automator.yaml coexistence verification
- OUT: fix-bug integration (Story 4), agent definitions (Stories 1, 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint dispatches RED test tasks (create-test, modify-existing-test) to `dso:red-test-writer` instead of general-purpose
  ← Satisfies: "Sprint orchestrators dispatch RED test tasks via direct subagent_type"
- When this story is complete, on writer failure the sprint orchestrator dispatches `dso:red-test-evaluator` with the failure message and task/story/epic context
  ← Satisfies: "handle the three-tier escalation protocol"
- When this story is complete, REVISE verdicts cause affected tasks to be requeued in the next batch; REJECT causes immediate retry with opus-tier RED agent; CONFIRM closes the task without implementation; retry failure escalates to user
  ← Satisfies: "sonnet attempt → opus evaluation → opus retry → user escalation"
- When this story is complete, the escalation protocol logic is implemented in a shared, reusable location (prompt template or library) that Story 4 can consume without duplication
  ← Satisfies: Maintainability concern from adversarial review
- When this story is complete, existing test-automator.yaml profile continues to correctly route non-RED test tasks; regression test cases in test-cases.yaml are updated to cover both RED task routing and non-RED test task routing
  ← Satisfies: Backward compatibility
- When this story is complete, sprint dispatch failure recovery (Phase 6 Step 0) does NOT fall back to general-purpose for RED test tasks — failures route through the escalation protocol instead
  ← Satisfies: Reliability concern from adversarial review
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Architecture] classify-task.py uses keyword scoring via YAML profiles, not task-type matching — determine interception mechanism (new profile vs pre-classification override) and ensure it outscores the existing test-automator profile for RED test tasks
- [Reliability] REVISE verdict modifies tickets mid-sprint — could conflict with concurrent batch tasks that depend on the revised task
- [Reliability] Sprint dispatch failure recovery (Phase 6 Step 0) currently falls back to general-purpose on dispatch failure — this would silently route RED tasks back to change-detector-producing agents
- [Reliability] classify-task.py change affects all task routing — verify non-RED tasks still route correctly
- [Maintainability] Sprint and fix-bug share the same escalation protocol — produce a shared template to prevent divergence
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
